steps.title: Find the driving concern in the students' claim
steps.content: Students claim opposition stems from concerns that car traffic would diminish Cuba Street's distinctive character. Find the quote directly reflecting this worry.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice C describes Cuba Street as “unusual” and expresses fear it will “become just like any other street”—directly showing concern about losing the street's distinctive identity. The other choices address consultation, business impacts, and pollution—not character concerns. That matches choice C — C is correct.
